text: Continuous integration is a software development practice where team members frequently merge their code changes into a shared repository. Each integration is verified by an automated build and a series of tests. In a typical CI pipeline, whenever a developer pushes new code, the system automatically compiles the program and runs unit and integration tests. This process helps catch issues early, before they become bigger problems in the main codebase. By detecting bugs quickly, teams can fix them more easily, saving time and effort. Overall, CI encourages faster collaboration and ensures that the software remains stable throughout development.
